member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClient
member.summary: Initiates communication and makes requests of server device.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClient.TryGetClientFunction``2(System.Byte,NModbus.Functions.IClientFunction{``0,``1}@)
member.summary: Attempt to retrieve a client function.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClientTransport
member.summary: The transport for a Modbus client.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClientTransport.SendAsync(NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a message but don't want for a response.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClientTransport.SendAndReceiveAsync(NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a message and wait for a response.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit
member.summary: There isn't a term in the Modbus spec that refers to the combination of a Unit Identifier with a Protocol Data Unit. An Application Data Unit can contain this and more, but is transport implementation specific. Hence - the term "Modbus Data Unit."
member.remarks: The term "ModbusMessage" was used initially, but this clashed with the concept of request / response messages.

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit.ProtocolDataUnit
member.summary: The Protocol Data Unit.

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit.UnitIdentifier
member.summary: The Unit Identifier.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServer
member.summary: Processes requests and return an appropriate response (or error message)

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServer.UnitIdentifier
member.summary: Gets the unit number for this server device.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServer.ProcessRequestAsync(NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Process the request.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServerNetwork
member.summary: A network of Modbus servers.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServerNetwork.TryAddServer(NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServer)
member.summary: Attempts to add a server to this network.
member.returns: True if the server was added, false otherwise.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServerNetwork.TryRemoveServer(System.Byte)
member.summary: Attempts to remove the Modbus server from this network.
member.returns: True if the server was removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusServerNetwork.ProcessRequestAsync(NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusDataUnit,NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusClientTransport,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Processes a request from a Modbus Server network.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusStream
member.summary: Represents a stream resource.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusStream.ReadAsync(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads a number of bytes from the input buffer.
member.param: The byte array to write the collected data to.
The offset in the buffer array to begin writing.
The number of bytes to read.
member.returns: The number of bytes that were actually read.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IModbusStream.WriteAsync(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes the specified number of bytes.
member.param: Byte array containing the data to write.
The offset at which to begin writing
The number of bytes to write.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.IStreamFactory
member.summary: Responsible for creating streams.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.IStreamFactory.CreateAndConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Create a connection and open that connection.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.IllegalFunction
member.summary: The function code received in the query is not an allowable action for the server. This may be because the function code is only applicable to newer devices, and was not implemented in the unit selected. It could also indicate that the server is in the wrong state to process a request of this type, for example because it is unconfigured and is being asked to return register values.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.IllegalDataAddress
member.summary: The data address received in the query is not an allowable address for the server. More specifically, the combination of reference number and transfer length is invalid. For a controller with 100 registers, the PDU addresses the first register as 0, and the last one as 99. If a request is submitted with a starting register address of 96 and a quantity of registers of 4, then this request will successfully operate (address-wise at least) on registers 96, 97, 98, 99. If a request is submitted with a starting register address of 96 and a quantity of registers of 5, then this request will fail with Exception Code 0x02 “Illegal Data Address” since it attempts to operate on registers 96, 97, 98, 99 and 100, and there is no register with address 100.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.IllegalDataValue
member.summary: A value contained in the query data field is not an allowable value for server. This indicates a fault in the structure of the remainder of a complex request, such as that the implied length is incorrect. It specifically does NOT mean that a data item submitted for storage in a register has a value outside the expectation of the application program, since the MODBUS protocol is unaware of the significance of any particular value of any particular register.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.ServerDeviceFailure
member.summary: An unrecoverable error occurred while the server was attempting to perform the requested action.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.Acknowledge
member.summary: Specialized use in conjunction with programming commands. The server has accepted the request and is processing it, but a long duration of time will be required to do so. This response is returned to prevent a timeout error from occurring in the client. The client can next issue a Poll Program Complete message to determine if processing is completed.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.ServerDeviceBusy
member.summary: Specialized use in conjunction with programming commands. The server is engaged in processing a long– duration program command. The client should retransmit the message later when the server is free.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.MemoryParityError
member.summary: Specialized use in conjunction with function codes 20 and 21 and reference type 6, to indicate that the extended file area failed to pass a consistency check. The server attempted to read record file, but detected a parity error in the memory. The client can retry the request, but service may be required.

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.GatewayPathUnavailable
member.summary: Specialized use in conjunction with gateways, indicates that the gateway was unable to allocate an internal communication path from the input port to the output port for processing the request. Usually means that the gateway is misconfigured or overloaded

member.name: F:NModbus.Interfaces.ModbusExceptionCode.GatewayTargetDeviceFailedToRespond
member.summary: Specialized use in conjunction with gateways, indicates that no response was obtained from the target device. Usually means that the device is not present on the network.

member.name: T:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit
member.summary: The MODBUS protocol defines a simple protocol data unit (PDU) independent of the underlying communication layers.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.#ctor(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte})
member.summary: Constructs a from a raw binary buffer.

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.#ctor(System.Byte,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Constructs a from a function code and data payload.

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.FunctionCode
member.summary: The function code.

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.Data
member.summary: The data part of this .

member.name: P:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.Length
member.summary: Gets the full length of this .

member.name: M:NModbus.Interfaces.ProtocolDataUnit.ToArray
member.summary: Gets a byte array representation of the PDU.
